_meta.unit: Early Task 4 (big-five sweep, house 2 of 5) — Dior lexicon: models + materials/signature-techniques + named seasonal capsules/art editions

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A)
types.material: signature technique / leather / canvas — the qualifier vocabulary (Cannage, Oblique, Toile de Jouy)
types.color: named seasonal capsule or numbered art edition (Dior does NOT publish a unique name per Lady Dior seasonal colorway)

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. Model and technique kept as separate facts. The honest finding: unlike Hermes (color codes) or Chanel (season codes), Dior does not name each Lady Dior seasonal colorway; the genuine named-seasonal layer is its capsules (Lady Art #1-#10, My ABCDior, Toile de Jouy, Dior Gradient/Ombre, Dioramour) — captured here, not invented. Debut-year conflict flagged: Bag Religion says ChouChou 1999 AND gifted to Diana 1995 (inconsistent); Christie's + Vogue date the Lady Dior to 1995, logged 1995 high. dior.com NOT scraped (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV run); newsroom queued for owner-present Chrome path. Live cultural fact: Jonathan Anderson named Dior Creative Director 2025, succeeding Maria Grazia Chiuri (2016-25).

coverage.colors: 9 named capsules / numbered art editions (Lady Art #1/#2/#5/#10 + capsule labels)

name: Lady Dior
note: Structured boxy top-handle, Cannage quilting, hanging D.I.O.R. charms. Born 1995 as a custom (orig. 'Chouchou'/'Chou Chou') gifted to Princess Diana, renamed for her; Gianfranco Ferre era. Christie's + Vogue both date 1995; Bag Religion's '1999' is a single-source error.

name: Lady D-Lite
note: Embroidered-canvas soft sister of the Lady Dior (Oblique/Toile de Jouy), same shape + charms.

name: Lady D-Joy
note: Lower, more elongated Cannage Lady Dior cut; D.I.O.R. charms read along the front. Debut year (~2021) is established-beat, not firmly resourced; Christie's catalogues some Lady D-Joy lots dated 2016 (likely re-dating).

name: Lady 95.22
note: 95.22 = 1995 creation + 2022 revival; new D-shaped handle and CD signature clasp. Also on dior.com newsroom (search snippet, not scraped).

name: Saddle
note: Galliano's curved saddle-shape flap with stirrup-D; SS2000 RTW collection, 1999. Y2K It-bag; revived 2018 by Chiuri. Christie's confirms 1999.

name: Book Tote
note: Flat open embroidered tote, a 'canvas' for Oblique/Toile/personalisation; SS2018 (Chiuri), based on a 1967 Bohan sketch. Christie's confirms 2018.

name: 30 Montaigne
note: Structured flap with antique-gold 'CD' clasp; named for Dior's 30 Avenue Montaigne address. Christie's dates lots to 2019.

name: Montaigne 30 / Montaigne (line)
note: Broader 30 Montaigne family label (bag, pouch, box, hobo variants). Same line as '30 Montaigne'; kept as an alias row for the categorizer.

name: Caro
note: Chain-strap Cannage flap with twist-CD clasp; named after Christian Dior's sister Catherine ('Caro'). Christie's dates the Caro to 2021.

name: Bobby
note: Crescent shoulder flap, saddle-style clasp on a wide strap; named for Christian Dior's dog Bobby. 'Dior Bobby' = same model. East-West variant ~2021.

name: Dior Vibe / D-Vibe
note: Sporty drawstring/zip bag with chunky CD-signature strap. Christie's catalogues a 'D-Vibe' Oblique lot dated 2024; line marketed from ~2022.

name: Diorama
note: Geometric chain flap with slide-lock 'CD' clasp; Raf Simons era. Christie's dates Diorama lots to 2015.

name: Diorever
note: Top-handle trapeze flap with turn-lock 'CD' plaque; Chiuri's early signature. Artnet names it among Dior icons; ~2016 debut is established beat.

name: Be Dior
note: Soft top-handle flap with flip-lock and rolled handle; Raf Simons era 'it' bag. ~2014 debut is established beat, not firmly resourced this run.

name: Dioraddict
note: Flap-and-chain bag, often denim/embroidered; mid-2010s. Christie's catalogues a Dioraddict lot dated 2017.

name: Diorissimo (tote)
note: Structured open tote with winged sides + Cannage charms. ~2012 debut (established beat); Christie's catalogues a Diorissimo 37 lot dated 2013. NOTE: 'Diorissimo' is also a heritage canvas name — see materials.

name: Dior Key
note: Soft North-South shoulder/tote with a key-charm; Chiuri era. ~2022 debut is established beat, not firmly resourced.

name: Toujours
note: Structured Macrocannage tote with rolled handles; 2020s launch (~2023, established beat, not firmly resourced).

name: Miss Dior (bag)
note: Cannage chain flap (a bag line, NOT the fragrance, NOT the 1950s licensed 'Miss Dior' stamp). Modern Miss Dior bag revived under Chiuri ~2023; established beat.

name: Nolita
note: Calfskin shoulder flap, a quieter 2024 launch. Christie's catalogues a Nolita lot dated 2024.

name: Cannage
note: Signature diamond quilting, inspired by the woven-cane Napoleon III chairs Monsieur Dior used at his 1947 shows; codified on the Lady Dior 1995. The most identifying Dior leather code.

name: Macrocannage
note: Oversized Cannage quilting (larger diamonds), 2020s. Whether 'Macrocannage' is an official Dior term vs reseller shorthand is unconfirmed; queued for a dior.com craft-page check.

name: Micro-Cannage
note: Fine, small-scale Cannage quilting, 2020s. Official-term status unconfirmed; queued.

name: Dior Oblique
note: Diagonal repeating 'Dior' jacquard monogram. Created 1967 by Marc Bohan; runway debut SS1969; major resurgence under Kim Jones (Dior Men) from 2018. Blue is signature; also grey/black/pink colorways. Vogue confirms Bohan 1967.

name: Toile de Jouy
note: House pastoral toile (animals + foliage), a Chiuri-era signature revived ~2019. Rotates colors (Pink, Navy, Grey) and prints ('Around the World', 'Sauvage'/Reverse).

name: Diorissimo (canvas)
note: Heritage Dior monogram canvas (DISTINCT from the Diorissimo tote model). Seen on vintage Diorissimo wallets/bags.

name: Cannage lambskin
note: Cannage quilting on supple lambskin — the classic, softest Lady Dior leather, core since 1995.

name: Cannage grained calfskin
note: Cannage on grained calfskin — sturdier, more scratch-resistant than lambskin; the durable Lady Dior alternative.

name: Patent / nubuck / exotics
note: Lady Dior also in patent calf, nubuck, and (limited) croc, python, ostrich, lizard, karung, tweed, velvet. Exotic editions fetch resale premiums; Christie's catalogues many.

name: D-Royaume / embroideries
note: Heavily embroidered/beaded/sequined/raffia/denim treatments (incl. Lady Art capsules, 'Graphic Cannage' AW24). 'D-Royaume' could not be isolated as a distinct sourced Dior term this run — logged as a lead, not a fact.

name: Dior Lady Art #1
note: First edition, launched for Art Basel Miami Beach 2016; 7 British-American artists (Ian Davenport, Marc Quinn, et al.).

name: Dior Lady Art #2
note: Christie's catalogues a 'Lady Art 2' medium Lady Dior dated 2017 (e.g. by Jamilla Okubo).

name: Dior Lady Art #5
note: Christie's catalogues 'Lady Art 5' editions dated 2020 (e.g. by Olga Titus, Recycle Group).

name: Dior Lady Art #10
note: Tenth edition, 10 artists, Rizzoli monograph + podcast; marks 30 years of the Lady Dior; first cycle in the Jonathan Anderson era. Editions #3/#4/#6-#9 (2018,2019,2021-24) exist by the annual cadence but were not each line-item sourced — queued.

name: My ABCDior
note: Personalisation program: Lady Dior with removable enamel letter-charm 'badges'; rotates seasonal charm colors. ~2018 onward (established beat).

name: Toile de Jouy (capsule)
note: Seasonal Toile prints across Book Tote/D-Lite/Saddle (Pink, Navy, Grey, 'Around the World', 'Sauvage'/Reverse).

name: Dior Gradient / Ombre
note: Degrade/ombre-dyed Cannage + exotic editions; e.g. 'Espresso Ombre' alligator 30 Montaigne (2020), 'green & blue tie-dye' alligator Lady Dior (2020), per Christie's.

name: Dioramour
note: Heart-motif Cannage capsule released around Valentine's / Lunar New Year. Established beat; year not pinned this run.

name: Graphic Cannage (AW24)
note: Raffia + denim Lady Dior with an enlarged graphic Cannage, AW2024 by Maria Grazia Chiuri (Dior craftsmanship film).
